data.title: ASOS Pretax Loss Narrows as Restructuring Efforts Continue
data.description:
(07:02 GMT) *ASOS PLC FY Rev GBP2.48B
(07:02 GMT) *ASOS PLC FY Oper Loss GBP212.3M
(07:02 GMT) *ASOS PLC FY Pretax Loss GBP281.6M
(07:04 GMT) *ASOS PLC FY Net Loss GBP298.4M
(07:04 GMT) *ASOS PLC FY Loss/Shr 250.1p
(07:05 GMT) *ASOS PLC FY Pre-Items, Pre-Tax Loss GBP98.2M
(07:06 GMT) *ASOS PLC FY25 Adj Ebitda GBP131.6M
(07:06 GMT) *Analysts Saw ASOS FY Adj Ebitda at GBP138M
(07:08 GMT) *ASOS PLC Issues FY26 View
(07:09 GMT) *ASOS PLC Sees Gross Merchandise Volume 3-4ppts Ahead of Rev in FY26
(07:10 GMT) *ASOS PLC Sees Gross Margin Improvement of at Least 100bps
(07:10 GMT) *ASOS PLC Sees Adj Ebitda Between GBP150M and GBP180M
(07:11 GMT) *ASOS PLC Expects Broadly Neutral Free Cash Flow
(07:43 GMT) ASOS Pretax Loss Narrows as Restructuring Efforts Continue

By Andrea Figueras

ASOS reported a narrowed pretax loss for its fiscal year as it undergoes a restructuring against the backdrop of a highly competitive fashion market and soft consumer sentiment.

The London-listed online fashion retailer said Friday that its pretax loss narrowed to 281.6 million pounds ($368.1 million) for the year ended Aug. 31 from a loss of 379.3 million pounds in the prior year. Revenue dropped 15% to 2.48 billion pounds.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization jumped nearly 52% on year to 131.6 million pounds.

The group embarked on a turnaround plan in fiscal 2022 under Chief Executive Officer Jose Antonio Ramos Calamonte. "Three years later, the turnaround is well progressed," the CEO said. The business has rebuilt its foundations and sharpened its focus, he said.

For fiscal 2026, the company expects gross merchandise volume--a key topline indicator--to be between 3 and 4 percentage points ahead of revenue. It also targets a gross margin of 48% to 50%, while adjusted Ebitda should come in a range of 150 million to 180 million pounds.

The priority for the year is to deepen the relationships with customers, the CEO said.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: H100 Group has converted SEK 122.5 million of its outstanding convertible loans
data.description:
THIS PRESS RELEASE MAY NOT BE RELEASED, PUBLISHED OR DISTRIBUTED, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY IN OR INTO THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, AUSTRALIA, JAPAN, NEW ZEALAND, SOUTH AFRICA, SOUTH KOREA, SWITZERLAND, CANADA, HONG KONG, BELARUS, RUSSIA OR IN ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE SUCH RELEASE, PUBLISHING OR DISTRIBUTION OF THIS PRESS RELEASE WOULD BE UNLAWFUL OR REQUIRE REGISTATION OR OTHER MEASURES IN ADDITION TO THOSE REQUIRED BY SWEDISH LAW. SEE SECTION "IMPORTANT INFORMATION" AT THE END OF THIS PRESS RELEASE.

H100 Group AB (“H100 Group” or the “Company”) announces that approximately SEK 122.5 million of its outstanding convertible loans have been converted to shares in accordance with the terms of the loan agreements at a conversion price of SEK 8.48 per share.

Following the conversions, the remaining outstanding nominal amount of the Company’s convertible debt is approximately SEK 219.8 million. The remaining convertible loans carry zero percent interest and mature in 2030. As a result of the conversions, the number of shares and votes in the Company increases by 14,450,468, from 320,799,769 to 335,250,237. The Company’s share capital increases by SEK 1,445,046.80, from SEK 32,079,976.90 to SEK 33,525,023.70.

Important information

data.title: Japan regional governor to approve restart of world's biggest nuclear power plant
data.description:
Key points:
  • Niigata governor to approve partial restart of Kashiwazaki-Kariwa nuclear power plant
  • TEPCO is planning to restart Units No. 6 and No. 7, two biggest at the plant
  • Restart a major step for Japan to strengthen energy security, lower energy import costs
  • PM Takaichi endorses more nuclear energy roll outs

By Katya Golubkova and Yuka Obayashi

A Japanese regional governor said on Friday he would allow a partial restart of the Kashiwazaki-Kariwa nuclear power plant, the world's biggest, as Japan tries to revive its nuclear sector and reduce fossil fuel imports.

Approval by Niigata Prefecture Governor Hideyo Hanazumi will remove the last major hurdle for plant operator Tokyo Electric Power Co (TEPCO) to go ahead with plans to restart one or two of Kashiwazaki-Kariwa's biggest reactors.

Hanazumi will still need to seek the prefectural assembly's vote of confidence on his decision during its regular session beginning on December 2.

A restart would be the first for TEPCO since the March 2011 tsunami destroyed its Fukushima Daiichi power plant. It would also be a breakthrough for Japan, which after the disaster shut all 54 nuclear reactors in operation at the time, leaving it heavily reliant on fossil fuel imports vulnerable to production shocks and supply disruptions.

Niigata residents remain divided between those supporting the restart and those opposing it, Hanazumi said, adding that providing accurate information about safety measures should help to raise awareness among those living in the area.

Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, who took office last month, has said she supports more nuclear relaunches to strengthen energy security and to address the cost of imported energy, which accounts for 60% to 70% of Japan's electricity generation. Japan spent 10.7 trillion yen ($68 billion) last year on imported liquefied natural gas and coal, a tenth of its total import costs.

"The restart ... is extremely important from the perspective of reducing ... electricity prices, and securing decarbonized power sources," Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said on Friday.

Of the 54 reactors in operation pre-Fukushima, Japan has restarted 14 of the 33 that remain operable.

TEPCO is planning to bring online units No. 6 and No. 7, which together can produce 2,710 megawatts of electricity, or about a third of Kashiwazaki-Kariwa's total capacity of 8,212 MW. TEPCO has said it plans to decommission some of the other five units at the facility.

In October, TEPCO finished checks at the No. 6 reactor after fuel loading, saying at the time that it had confirmed the main systems required for reactor startup were operating properly.

Commodity analysts at Kpler have already lowered their estimates for LNG imports by Japan, the world's second-biggest buyer after China, by 3 million metric tons to 63 million tons for next year on potential nuclear restarts.

In July, Kansai Electric Power , Japan's top nuclear power operator, said it would begin conducting surveys to investigate building a reactor in western Japan that would be the first new unit since the Fukushima disaster.

TEPCO shares closed 1.9% down on Friday, paring losses after Japan's nuclear watchdog said on Thursday that confidential documents were mishandled at the plant. That was better than a 2.4% drop in the Nikkei index .

TEPCO continues to pay compensation for the Fukushima Daiichi disaster of 2011, the world's worst nuclear power accident since Chernobyl in 1986.

($1 = 157.1800 yen)

data.title: Fitch Assigns Hubei Huanchuan State-Owned Capital Investment First-Time 'BBB-' IDR; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Hong Kong-21 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has assigned China-based Hubei Huanchuan State-Owned Capital Investment Operation Group Co., Ltd. (HCSCI) first-time Long-Term Foreign- and Local-Currency Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) of 'BBB-'. The Outlook is Stable.

The rating reflects the Xiaogan government's support for HCSCI and HCSCI's strong government linkage, given its important role as Xiaogan's main urban development platform and one of its key government-related entities (GREs). The Stable Outlook reflects our expectation that the creditworthiness of the municipality and the support score will remain steady.

Fitch applies the Government-Related Entities Rating Criteria to rate HCSCI, despite the indirect government ownership. We "look through" the immediate parent, Hubei Huanchuan Capital Investment Co., Ltd (HCCI), as HCSCI is treated as a public policy entity with strong government links. Fitch believes support is likely to ultimately flow from the government. HCCI is wholly owned by Xiaogan through the Xiaogan State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission (SASAC).

HCSCI's Standalone Credit Profile (SCP) of 'b' is derived under our Public Policy, Revenue-Supported Entities Rating Criteria.

Key Rating Drivers

Support Score Assessment 'Extremely Likely'

We believe extraordinary support from the Xiaogan government to HCSCI would be 'Extremely likely' in case of need, reflecting a support score of 35 out of a maximum 60 under our Government-Related Entities Rating Criteria. This reflects our assessment of the government's responsibility and incentive to support the company.

Responsibility to Support

Decision Making and Oversight 'Very Strong'

HCSCI is tightly controlled and wholly owned by the Xiaogan SASAC through HCCI. We look through HCCI as the Xiaogan government is the ultimate, effective shareholder and exercises strict control. This includes defining HCSCI's key business scope and developing strategics, approving annual bond-issuance plans, overseeing annual investment plans and major projects, appointing the board of directors except for the employee representative, governing profit distribution, monitoring operational results, and conducting performance evaluations.

HCSCI submits quarterly reports to the government that cover key investments, financing activities, and asset, resource and fund management. Its urban development projects - such as the Laohuan River improvement and resettlement housing - are highly strategic.

Precedents of Support 'Strong'

The government has provided material support to enable HCSCI to fulfil its policy mandate and maintain financial profile. Cash, equity and asset injections totalled CNY28.13 billion in 2020-2024, equivalent to 57.4% of HCSCI's end-2024 equity, and comprised stakes in local GREs as well as land and property. The support also included subsidies amounting to CNY979 million over the same period, representing 58.7% of total net profit. The subsidies are mainly for public transportation and urban development projects.

Incentive to Support

Preservation of Government Policy Role 'Strong'

HCSCI is responsible for infrastructure construction, social housing and public transport in Xiaogan, and is the sole water supplier. A default by HCSCI would hinder the implementation of the government's development strategies, as delays to its projects would affect future construction and the operation of public-welfare projects over the long term. Nevertheless, Fitch notes that a HCSCI default would not have an immediate impact on existing infrastructure, which constrains the support assessment from being higher.

Contagion Risk 'Strong'

We consider HCSCI a high-profile GRE in Xiaogan, given its role as the key urban developer and the largest GRE by total assets. It is an active issuer with diversified funding channels and established capital-market access. Borrowings primarily finance policy-driven projects and are supported by long-term loans from major commercial and policy banks. Bonds represented around 43% of total debt at end-2024. Fitch believes a default by HCSCI would most likely disrupt financing access or increase funding costs for the municipal government and its other GREs.

The company faces no financial pressure. Fitch believes a default by HCSCI would almost certainly damage the Xiaogan government's credibility and significantly impair long-term financing access for the city's other GREs. However, a higher assessment is constrained by the visibility of other active GRE issuers. Our assessment has also factored in the GRE dynamics in the region.

Standalone Credit Profile

The 'b' Standalone Credit Profile (SCP) is derived from a 'Midrange' risk profile and 'b' financial profile. We position the SCP at the middle of the 'b' category as its leverage is at the mid-range of the 'b' category in comparison with similar urban developer peers. Fitch believes the comfortable liquidity to be an important mitigant despite HCSCI's high leverage.

Risk Profile: 'Midrange'

Our risk profile assessment reflects 'Midrange' assessments for revenue, expenditure, and liabilities and liquidity risk.

Revenue Risk: 'Midrange'

The revenue risk assessment reflects 'Midrange' demand and pricing characteristics. HCSCI has diversified revenue sources, with some geographic and customer concentrations in Xiaogan. We expect demand to be supported by the municipality's solid economic growth. The company's existing project pipeline is also sufficient to support its business growth. Pricing for most of HCSCI's businesses is based on government contracts and supported by the government's subsidy arrangement.

Expenditure Risk: 'Midrange'

The assessment reflects HCSCI's 'Midrange' operating costs and supply risk, and investment planning. HCSCI has well-identified cost drivers. The supply of resources and labour is adequate with limited constraints, considering the city's solid economic growth and proximity to the provincial capital, Wuhan. Major investment plans align with the municipal government's objectives, and execution has been strong. However, flexibility to alter major capex or pass through costs is limited, as these are set and regulated by the government.

Liabilities and Liquidity Risk: 'Midrange'

Our assessment is based on 'Midrange' debt and liquidity characteristics. The company has a well-balanced maturity profile, with a weighted-average maturity of around 7.9 years at end-2024. Short-term debt accounted for 24.5% of total debt, but refinancing risk is mitigated by the long average maturities and well-dispersed repayment schedules. We believe HCSCI maintains strong access to bond markets and sufficient liquidity to meet debt service, supported by solid relationships with major Chinese banks.

Financial Profile 'b'

Fitch assesses the financial profile in the 'b' category, using the leverage ratio (net debt/EBITDA) as the primary metric. We believe the ratio will increase gradually to above 65x in 2029 from 54.4x in 2025, driven by capex for urban development and the government's slow pace of repayment.

PEER ANALYSIS

We view municipal-level GREs, including Jingzhou Municipal Urban Development Holding Group Co.,Ltd (JMUD, BBB-/Stable), Deyang Development Holding Co., Ltd. (DYDH, BBB-/Stable) as comparable peers, as they are also municipal-level GREs with similar gross regional product. JMUD and DYDH are also the largest urban developers for their respective regions and responsible for social housing development, with support scores of 35.

Issuer Profile

HCSCI, established in 2017, is the main GRE in Xiaogan municipality. It is the key platform for comprehensive urban development, including infrastructure construction, social housing, water supply and public transportation. It has also engaged in trading, sale of sand and gravel materials, and labour subcontracting.

Key Assumptions

Our rating case is a through-the-cycle scenario that incorporates a combination of revenue, cost and financial risk stresses. It is based on HCSCI's 2020-2024 historical figures and 2025-2029 scenario assumptions.

- Average operating revenue decrease of 0.2% a year under the rating case in 2025-2029, primarily because subsidiary Xiaogan Gaochuang Investment Co., Ltd was transferred out in 2025. Excluding this effect, revenue will grow moderately at 2.7% during 2026-2029.

- Average operating expenditure decrease of 0.2% a year under the rating case in 2025-2029, reflecting steady increase in recurring expenditure, with variable costs fluctuating alongside revenue growth.

- Average net capex of CNY3.2 billion in 2025-2029, similar to the historical average of CNY3.6 billion in 2020-2024, which is aligned with the project pipeline and will mainly fund the company's urban development projects.

RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

- A lowering of Fitch's credit view of the Xiaogan government's ability to provide subsidies, grants or other legitimate resources allowed under China's policies and regulations.

- Deterioration in the overall support score to 32.5 points or below.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

- An improvement in Fitch's credit view of the Xiaogan government's ability to provide subsidies, grants or other legitimate resources allowed under China's policies and regulations.

- Improvement in the overall support score to 45 points or above.

Date of Relevant Committee 13-Nov-2025 REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

Hubei Huanchuan State-Owned Capital Investment Operation Group Co., Ltd.; Long Term Issuer Default Rating; New Rating; BBB-; Rating Outlook Stable

data.title: Yomiuri: DBJ President Says Impact of U.S. Tariff Policies on Japan 'Smaller than Expected' Despite Remaining Uncertainties
data.description:
By Kentaro Otsuka

Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

The impact of U.S. tariff policies on the Japanese economy has so far been "smaller than expected," said Seiji Jige, president of the government-affiliated Development Bank of Japan (DBJ), on Wednesday.

"The impact was smaller than we had originally expected, but uncertainty remains," Jige said at a press conference on the day.

Despite being affected by the U.S. tariff policies, many listed companies showed solid performance during the April-September period, according to their financial results for the first half of fiscal 2025, which were released through mid-November.

"Our clients' financial results were good," Jige said. However, he also noted that the burden on companies is increasing because they must create extra procurement networks as U.S. tariff policies have disrupted existing supply chains.

The Japanese government has pledged to invest about 500 billion dollars (about 80 trillion yen) in the United States, a commitment made during recent Japan-U.S. tariff negotiations. Jige said that the DBJ has not received a request from the Japanese government to provide financial support for the investment. However, he added that the bank "will actively respond if we receive requests from clients."

Jige was asked how much the increased U.S. investment will influence capital investment in Japan. "I don't think (the increase) will hinder (capital investment)," Jige said. Rather, investments were more likely to have shifted from Japan to the United States during the administration of former U.S. President Joe Biden because of the Inflation Reduction Act, Jige said. "President (Donald) Trump's actions are centered on traditional manufacturing sectors," he said.

Jige also commented on the recent success of Japanese chipmaker Rapidus Corp. of developing a prototype of a 2-nanometer semiconductor, as the DBJ has expressed its willingness to invest in the chipmaker. "It is a remarkable achievement," Jige said. "It's wonderful that they have developed it on time."

However, companies capable of fully utilizing cutting-edge semiconductors are lacking in Japan, which could be a future challenge for Rapidus, Jige said. "Value chains have not yet been established in Hokkaido (where Rapidus' plant is located)," he said. "It will be crucial for parts manufacturers to be set up (in Hokkaido)."

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-M0000159576-1

data.title: Subgen AI AB: First day of trading under new ticker SUBGEN on Nasdaq First North Growth Market
data.description:
Today, Friday 21 November 2025, marks the first day of trading in Subgen AI AB’s (“Subgen AI” or the “Company”) ordinary shares under the new ticker SUBGEN on Nasdaq First North Growth Market. The ordinary shares currently trade under ISIN code SE0014401121, which will be replaced by the ISIN code SE0026820854 following the planned reverse share split. As of today, the Company’s listed warrants of series TO 5 and TO 6 will also trade under the tickers SUBGEN TO 5 and SUBGEN TO 6, respectively.

“Today marks an important milestone as Subgen AI begins trading under its new ticker on Nasdaq First North Growth Market. As AI adoption accelerates across Europe, our focus on delivering scalable, sovereign AI solutions is more relevant than ever. We appreciate the strong backing from our shareholders and look forward to advancing our profitable growth strategy from Sweden,” said Lorenzo Serratosa, CEO of Subgen AI.

For further information, please contact:

Lorenzo Serratosa, CEO Subgen AI AB

Email: info@subgen.ai

The Company’s Certified Adviser is Eminova Fondkommission AB | adviser@eminova.se

About Subgen AI

Subgen AI is an enterprise artificial intelligence company that develops both infrastructure and proprietary agent-centric AI-as-a-Service software, branded as Serenity Star. Its solutions are designed to scale and accelerate the adoption of AI across organizations. Subgen AI is experiencing rapid growth, building data centers and delivering services to clients in sectors such as healthcare, energy, legal, and human resources. Subgen AI operates across Europe, Latin America, and the United States. For more information, see Subgen AI’s website https://subgen.ai/.

Notes:

1.

Rule 8 of the Takeover Code (the "Code") sets out the circumstances in which Dealing Disclosures and/or Opening Position Disclosures are required to be made by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them, persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more and exempt principal traders ("EPTs") connected with the parties to an offer.

2.

Where an offeror is marked "Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required", dealings and positions in relevant securities of that offeror are not required to be disclosed under Rule 8. This is either because it has been announced that the offer or possible offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash or because disclosure is not required for some other reason.

3.

Dealings and positions required to be disclosed under Rule 8 of the Code include dealings and positions in derivatives referenced to, and options in respect of, relevant securities of an offeree company and, where appropriate, an offeror (see Note 2 above).

4.

The information set out in the Disclosure Table relating to the relevant securities in issue of an offeree company or of an offeror (and any amendments thereto) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by such company under Rule 2.9 of the Code. Similarly, details of any deadline set under Rule 2.6 by which a potential offeror must announce either a firm intention to make a bid or that it does not intend to make an offer (or, in the case of Rule 2.6(a), by which the Panel must consent to an extension of the deadline) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by the offeree company.

5.

Additions and deletions of offeree companies and offerors are reflected in the Disclosure Table throughout the day, following the relevant announcement. Other amendments may also be made throughout the day. In any event, details of the classes of relevant securities issued by an offeree or offeror company, the number of such securities in issue ("NSI") and the International Securities Identification Number ("ISIN") of such securities announced under Rule 2.9 of the Code before 5.00 pm each day will be included in the final version of the Disclosure Table by 5.30 pm that day. The final version of the Disclosure Table issued each day will be marked "Final" and persons required to make Dealing Disclosures and Opening Position Disclosures are advised to use that final version. Any changes to the Rule 2.6 deadline of a potential offeror will also be reflected in the "Final" version of the Disclosure Table, although any such changes will not be shown in the "Amendments" section at the top of the Disclosure Table.

6.

Dealing Disclosures by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them and connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the business day (as defined in the Code) following the date of the relevant dealing and Dealing Disclosures by persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by 3.30 pm on the business day following the date of the relevant dealing.

7.

Opening Position Disclosures by the parties to an offer and by connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Where required, Opening Position Disclosures by persons with interests in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by no later than 3.30 pm on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Details of the time and date at which an offer period commenced and at which an offeror was first identified are set out in the Disclosure Table. In order to calculate the deadline for Opening Position Disclosures, it is therefore necessary to count forward 10 business days from the relevant date.

8.

Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service ("RIS").

9.

This Note is applicable if it says "See Note 9 below" by the name of the company in the Disclosure Table above.  The Executive has granted the company a dispensation from the requirements under the Code that announcements must be published via a RIS. The company is instead required to publish all announcements on its website. The Executive has also granted a dispensation from Note 3 on Rule 8 of the Code that disclosures made under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a RIS. Therefore, Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code may be made to the company at the address stated in the announcement that commenced the offer period.

10.

The following are approved Regulatory Information Services:

.

+44 (0)20 7626 1982

data.title: Raute's financial calendar and annual general meeting in 2026
data.description:
Raute Oyj

Raute Corporation, Stock Exchange Release, 21 November 2025 at 9:45 a.m. EET

Raute’s financial calendar and annual general meeting in 2026

In 2026, Raute will publish financial reports as follows:

  • Financial statements release for the year 2025 on Thursday, February 12, 2026
  • Business review January–March 2026 on Thursday, May 7, 2026
  • Half-year financial report January–June 2026 on Wednesday, August 12, 2026
  • Business review January–September 2026 on Friday, October 30, 2026.

Raute's Financial statements, the Board of Directors report including sustainability statement, Corporate governance statement, Remuneration report and Annual report 2025 will be published as a stock exchange release and on the company's website www.raute.com during week 11.

Annual General Meeting is tentatively scheduled to be held in Lahti on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Finnish time. The meeting will be convened later by Raute's Board of Directors.

FURTHER INFORMATION:

Ville Halttunen, CFO, tel. +358 50 3460868

DISTRIBUTION:

Nasdaq Helsinki Ltd, Main media, www.raute.com  

RAUTE IN BRIEF - Making Wood Matter

Raute is the partner to future-proof the wood industry. Our technologies cover different production processes with supporting digital and analytics solutions for engineered wood products. Additionally, we offer a full-scale service concept ranging from spare parts to regular maintenance and modernizations. Our innovative hardware and software solutions are designed to support our customers’ efficient consumption of natural resources. In mill-scale projects, Raute is a global market leader both in the plywood and LVL industries. Raute’s head office and main production plant are located in Lahti, Finland. The company’s other production plants are located in Kajaani, Finland, the Vancouver area of Canada, Changzhou, China, and in Pullman, WA, USA. Raute’s net sales in 2024 were EUR 204.6 million. The Group’s headcount at the end of 2024 was 783. More information about the company can be found at www.raute.com

https://news.cision.com/raute-oyj/r/raute-s-financial-calendar-and-annual-general-meeting-in-2026%2Cc4270702

data.title: Heidelberg Materials: Financial Details Weren't Disclosed
Hammerson Buys Remainder of The Oracle Stake in $137 Million Deal
data.description:
(07:03 GMT) *Hammerson PLC Buy The Oracle
(07:03 GMT) *Hammerson PLC Buys Remaining 50% Interest in Oracle, Reading
(07:04 GMT) *Hammerson PLC Says Year-To-Date Trading Performance is Strong
(07:04 GMT) *Hammerson PLC Raises View
(07:04 GMT) *Hammerson PLC Upgrades EPRA Earnings Guidance for FY25
(07:09 GMT) *Hammerson Bought 50% in Oracle From Its JV Partner for GBP104.5M
(07:10 GMT) *Hammerson: Acquisition Expected to Be 5% Accretive to Group's FY26 EPRA Earnings
(07:10 GMT) *Hammerson 3Q Like-for-like Footfall Was Up 10% Year-on-Year
(07:11 GMT) *Hammerson Year-to-date GRI Is Up 9%; NRI Up 20%
(07:11 GMT) *Hammerson Sees Additional Opportunities to Unlock Further Value
(07:11 GMT) *Hammerson Raises FY Total Gross Rental Income Growth Raised to 19%
(07:12 GMT) *Hammerson Had Guided for FY Total Gross Rental Income Growth of 17%
(07:12 GMT) *Hammerson FY Like-for-like Gross, Net Rental Income Growth Expected to Be Around 3%
(07:12 GMT) *Hammerson Sees FY EPRA Earnings of At Least GBP102M
(07:13 GMT) *Hammerson Had Seen FY EPRA Earnings of Around GBP101M
(07:13 GMT) *Hammerson Expects Further Growth in 2026 and 2027
(07:14 GMT) *Hammerson 3Q Like-for-like Footfall At Oracle Was Up 10% Year-on-Year
(07:14 GMT) *Hammerson 3Q Group Footfall Increased by 5% Year-on-Year
(07:15 GMT) *Hammerson UK 3Q Footfall, Like-For-Like Sales Year-On-Year Growth 6% and 3% Respectively
(07:15 GMT) *Hammerson: Demand for Space Is Robust
(07:15 GMT) *Hammerson Signed 261 Leases With GBP38M of Headline Rent
(07:16 GMT) *Hammerson Occupancy Is 95%, Expected to Improve With Ongoing Repositioning
(07:16 GMT) *Hammerson: Oracle Acquisition Funded From Existing Cash on Balance Sheet
(07:17 GMT) *Hammerson Continues to See Opportunities to Recycle Cap via Asset Rotation
(07:36 GMT) Hammerson Buys Remainder of The Oracle Stake in $137 Million Deal

By Adam Whittaker

Hammerson said it acquired the remaining 50% of The Oracle shopping centre from its joint-venture partner in a 104.5 million pound deal ($136.6 million), and raised its guidance.

The shopping centre operator acquired the remainder of the stake in the Reading shopping centre from a subsidiary of the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority.

Hammerson said it expects the acquisition to grow its 2026 EPRA earnings--a key metric for the industry--by 5%, with further growth expected in 2027.

The company also increased its 2025 total gross rental income guidance to 19%, from 17% previously. Like-for-like gross and net rental income growth is expected to be around 3%.

Meanwhile, its EPRA earnings forecast has been raised to at least 102 million pounds, from around 101 million pounds, it said.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: India's Tata Motors PV set for weekly loss on revised annual margin target
data.description:
** Shares of Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles decline 7.3% so far this week, set for their worst weekly performance since early-April, if losses hold

** TAMO stock on track for the biggest weekly drop in the benchmark Nifty 50 Index , which has climbed about 1% for the week

** Stock fell as much as 7.2% on Monday after co cut its fiscal 2026 margin target for Jaguar Land Rover due to a cyberattack disrupting its production

** TAMO stock is rated "hold" on avg by 29 brokerages, while rivals Maruti Suzuki and Hyundai Motor India are rated "buy", as per data compiled by LSEG

** Still, TAMO stock up 0.7% on the day

** YTD, TAMO down 19%

data.title: EQT to Buy Majority Stake in Filtration Services Provider Desotec from Blackstone
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

STOCKHOLM--EQT agreed to buy a majority stake in filtration services provider Desotec from Blackstone, it said Friday.

The Swedish buyout group said it is partnering with two German family offices, Athos and Merckle, who will co-invest alongside EQT, while Blackstone will remain a minority investor.

Belgium-based Desotec provides services that remove pollutants from air, water and soil.

EQT and Blackstone plan to support Desotec's continued growth across Europe and to accelerate its expansion in North America, where the market for mobile filtration is still in its early stages.

The transaction is subject to customary conditions and approvals and is expected to close during the first half of 2026.

Financial terms weren't disclosed.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Sugar maker Tereos sees profits plunge as prices hit new lows
data.description:
Tereos, one of the world's largest sugar makers, reported a plunge in profits for the first half of its 2025/26 financial year, reflecting continued weakness in sugar markets as prices hit multi-year lows on plentiful supplies.

For April to September recurring operating profit fell 95% to 16 million euros, as revenue dropped 19% to 2.62 billion euros ($3.02 billion) at current exchange rates.

Adjusted earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) fell 66% to 173 million euros.

The group had warned of a difficult start of the year when it posted its yearly results in May as prices continued to wane.

European sugar prices hit a three-year low in September, pressured by high sugar beet yields, strong sugar content, and falling global markets. Tereos' starch division was also weighed down by sharply lower prices.

Raw and white sugar futures slumped to a five-year low earlier this month on expectations of a substantial global surplus in the 2025/26 season.

This tough environment weighed on sector earnings across the industry, with Suedzucker , Europe's largest sugar producer, also reporting steep declines.

Tereos also posted a net loss of 572 million euros for the first half of 2025/26, including a 499 million euro goodwill depreciation, mainly from its European sugar division.

A weaker dollar during the period hurt Tereos' export competitiveness while supporting ethanol and alcohol imports, the company said.

Tereos' Brazilian division saw revenue fall 28% as poor weather reduced sugarcane volumes and sugar content, offsetting operational gains.

Tereos expects European sugar prices and the dollar to remain low in the second half, prompting it to raise its debt leverage forecast for 2025/26 to a temporary 6.0x from 5.0x. The ratio stood at 4.5x on September 30, with net debt at 2.1 billion euros, up from 2.0 billion a year earlier.

($1 = 0.8677 euros)

data.title: PRESSR: DEWA Women’s Committee and National Bonds host financial literacy workshop for female employees
data.description:
Dubai, UAE: The Dubai Electricity and Water Authority (DEWA) Women’s Committee  and National Bonds, the UAE’s Sharia-compliant savings and investment company, organised a workshop to empower DEWA’s female employees with essential financial knowledge. The sessions focused on the fundamentals of prudent saving, strategic investing and long-term financial sustainability. Through interactive discussions and practical examples, participants explored strategies for managing income, prioritising financial goals and navigating everyday economic challenges with balance and confidence.

“We are committed to empowering our female employees and equipping them with the necessary tools, expertise and knowledge to enhance their active participation in the labour market, decision-making, excellence and leadership. We collaborate with our national partners to open new horizons for creativity and innovation, ensuring that female employees remain pivotal partners to their male counterparts in driving economic prosperity. We are also pleased to contribute to instilling a culture of sound financial planning and reinforcing authentic Emirati values based on stability, balance, cohesion and the mindful avoidance of waste,” said Fatima Al Jokar, Chairperson of the Women’s Committee at DEWA.

Rehab Lootah, Group Deputy Chief Executive Officer of National Bonds, said: “Financial saving plays a pivotal role in building financial stability for families and communities. At National Bonds, we believe that empowering women with financial knowledge and skills will positively impact future generations. We are happy to collaborate with the Women’s Committee at DEWA to support women and enhance their role in family and community stability. We aim to equip women with the knowledge and expertise necessary to manage their finances wisely, ensuring they and their families can confidently and stably face financial challenges. This type of partnership opens new avenues for reaching diverse segments of society and contributes to building a more stable and sustainable financial future, in line with the UAE’s vision for a prosperous and financially secure society.”
